query: I've been thinking about adding a notification system to our app. Users should get alerts when their deployments finish, maybe via email or slack or something. Not sure about the best approach yet, the codebase uses both Redis pub/sub and a simple polling system in different places.

we need some kind of rate limiting for the API. right now theres nothing stopping someone from hammering /api/search and killing our elasticsearch cluster. im thinking maybe express-rate-limit but open to other ideas

I want to add a plugin system to this CLI tool so other devs can write extensions. Something like how VS Code does it maybe? The tool currently has a monolithic command structure in src/commands/ and I'm not sure how to make it extensible without breaking the existing commands.

Add support for multi-tenant data isolation. Each customer org should only see their own data. We're on postgres and currently everything is in shared tables with no tenant_id column. This is going to be a big change and I want to make sure we get the approach right before touching anything.

ok so I want to build a dashboard that shows real-time metrics from our k8s clusters. should pull from prometheus and display things like pod count, cpu/memory usage, error rates. not sure if we should use grafana embedding or build something custom with recharts. the frontend is next.js 14 with app router.

I'm thinking about replacing our REST API with GraphQL but I'm not sure if it makes sense for our use case. We have about 40 endpoints, most are simple CRUD but some have complex nested data fetching that results in the frontend making 5-6 requests per page load. Can you help me think through whether this is worth it?

Need to add PDF export to the reporting module. Users click a button, it generates a PDF of whatever report they're viewing. The reports are React components with charts (recharts) and tables. Not sure if we should do server-side rendering with puppeteer or use a client-side library like jspdf.

We want to add end-to-end encryption for our chat feature. Messages should be encrypted on the client before sending, stored encrypted on the server, and only decryptable by the intended recipients. We currently store messages in plaintext in postgres. I need help figuring out the key management approach.

I'd like to add a new command to cape that auto-generates a skill from a conversation transcript. Like if you just had a good workflow with Claude, you could run /cape:extract-skill and it would analyze the conversation and produce a SKILL.md

The login page is showing a 500 error when users try to sign in with their Google account. The error log shows 'TokenError: Bad Request' from passport.js. Started happening after we updated the oauth callback URL in the Google Cloud Console yesterday.

Can you refactor the UserService class? It's 800 lines long and handles authentication, profile management, notification preferences, and billing all in one file. I want it split into focused services. Tests are in tests/user-service.test.ts.
Run the test suite and fix any failures. I made some changes to the auth middleware in src/middleware/auth.ts and want to make sure nothing is broken.
The br epic for the caching layer is already created (EPIC-42). It has 3 tasks defined. Start executing Task 1 which is adding Redis connection pooling to src/cache/redis-client.ts.
Update the README to document the new CLI flags we added last sprint. The flags are --dry-run, --verbose, and --config-path. They're implemented in src/cli/args.ts.

The /api/users endpoint is returning results in 3.2 seconds. Profile it and find what's slow. My guess is the N+1 query in the permissions join but I'm not sure. The query is in src/repos/user-repo.ts:45.
Rename all instances of 'accessToken' to 'bearerToken' across the codebase. It's used in about 15 files. Make sure the database migration renames the column too.
Review PR #234 from @daniels. It adds WebSocket support to the notification system. Check if the implementation follows our existing patterns and leave inline comments on anything that looks off.

Add a --json flag to the existing /cape:changelog command so it outputs structured JSON instead of markdown. The command is already implemented in commands/changelog.md, just needs the flag option added.
What does the handleWebhook function in src/integrations/stripe.ts do? I need to understand the flow before I touch it for the upgrade to Stripe API v2024-12.
Commit the changes I just made and push to the feature branch.
